keyRules: Maximum altitude: 152 m (500 ft) above ground level (AGL)
Minimum distance of 4 km from airports and airfields
A minimum distance of 30 m horizontally and 20 m vertically from people, buildings, or objects
Drones weighing more than 2 kg: mandatory registration with the MTC via the Virtual Submission Desk
Commercial use (any weight): MTC registration required + specific authorization
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS)
Flight permitted only during the day — night flights require specific authorization from the DGAC
Urban areas: require prior MTC approval; rural areas: no specific restrictions
Restricted areas: airports, airfields, strategic facilities, and high-density population areas without permission
Legal distinction: recreational use (leisure) vs. professional/commercial use (specialized activities)
importantNotes: 📋 REGULATION NTC 001-2015: the primary regulatory framework for drones (RPAS) in Peru. It clearly distinguishes between recreational and professional/commercial use. The DGAC of the MTC is the competent authority.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: The rules apply equally to non-residents. For recreational drone flights with drones weighing less than 2 kg in rural areas, no prior registration is required. For commercial use, in urban areas, or near archaeological sites, the same permits are required as for Peruvian citizens.

🏛️ MACHU PICCHU AND ARCHAEOLOGICAL SITES: The Ministry of Culture prohibits the use of drones without special permission at Machu Picchu, the Nazca Lines, Chan Chan, Caral, Kuélap, and other archaeological sites. Authorization from the Ministry of Culture is required IN ADDITION to that from the MTC.

🌳 PROTECTED NATURAL AREAS AND THE AMAZON: SERNANP (National Service for Protected Natural Areas) requires a special permit for drones in parks such as Manu, Tambopata, and Huascarán. The Amazon and indigenous communities have additional restrictions.

🏙️ URBAN AREAS IN LIMA AND CUSCO: Downtown Lima (Plaza Mayor, Government Palace), Jorge Chávez Airport (LIM), downtown Cusco and the airport (CUZ), military zones, and embassies have permanent restrictions. The historic center of Cusco requires a permit from the Ministry of Culture.

primaryLinks.title: MTC — Requirements for Operating a Drone
primaryLinks.description: Official website of the Ministry of Transportation and Communications (MTC) listing the requirements for operating drones. It includes regulation NTC 001-2015 and DGAC procedures.

primaryLinks.title: MTC Virtual Filing Desk — RPAS Registration
primaryLinks.description: The MTC's official online platform for registering drones (RPAS) entirely online. Select "Registration of Remotely Piloted Aircraft Systems (RPAS)" and fill out the form

primaryLinks.title: MTC — Institutional Page
primaryLinks.description: Main website of the Ministry of Transportation and Communications. Access to procedures, regulations, and aviation services provided by the DGAC

primaryLinks.title: DGAC — RPAS Contact (Authorizations)
primaryLinks.description: DGAC's official email address for inquiries regarding RPAS and special authorizations: rpas-dgac@mtc.gob.pe. Phone: (01) 615 7800, ext. 1371

secondaryLinks.title: El Peruano — Drone Permits (Official)
secondaryLinks.description: El Peruano Official Gazette: Official information on the permits you need to operate drones, according to the MTC and the DGAC

secondaryLinks.title: TV Perú — Official Guide to Requirements
secondaryLinks.description: Official coverage by the public broadcaster TV Perú with updated information on the requirements for operating drones, as specified by the MTC

secondaryLinks.title: MTC Offices — In-Person Service
secondaryLinks.description: MTC Headquarters: 1203 Jr. Zorritos, Cercado de Lima. Services are also available at regional Citizen Service Centers for in-person RPAS procedures.

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the official sources of the DGAC of the Ministry of Transport and Communications (MTC) before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. Peru enforces Complementary Technical Standard (NTC) 001-2015 with simplified registration through the MTC’s Virtual Filing Desk.
